sections.paragraph:
ἔνειμι ptc. ἐνών; impf. 3 pl. ἐνῆσαν 3 Km 10:17 (s. εἰμί 1, in sense of ‘be, exist’; Hom. et al.; pap, LXX; TestSol 13:3 P; EpArist 285; Joseph.)

① to be inside an area or object, be in 2 Cl 19:2. τὰ ἐνόντα what is inside, the contents (Thu. 4, 57, 3 et al.; PTebt 414, 20 [II a.d.] τὸ σφυρίδιν [=-ιον] μετὰ τῶν ἐνόντων; Jos., Bell. 6, 183; Just., A II, 13, 5; Ath., R. 75, 24 al.) Lk 11:41.

② to be within the limits of realization, it is possible ἔνεστι impers. (Polyb. 21, 4, 14; 22, 14, 2; 4 Macc 4:22; Philo, Migr. Abr. 189: ὡς ἔνι μάλιστα=as much as is possible; Phalaris, Ep. 88 ὡς ἐνῆν; Jos., Ant. 1, 244; Just., D. 46, 2; Tat. 19, 2; Ath. 31, 2) IRo 4:1; Hs 8, 10, 2 v.l. Shortened to ἔνι, q.v.—M-M.

ptc. ptc. = participle

impf. impf. = imperfect

  1. pl. = plural

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

impers. impers. = impersonal(ly)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Phalaris Phalaris , tyrant of Acragas (VI b.c.), in whose name letters were written centuries later, II? a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 334
